channel.title: Tils on frn.sh
channel.description: Recent content in Tils on frn.sh

item.title: Difference between shell source and export
item.description: Non-interactive shells don’t load initialization files, so bash -c 'declare -f' doesn’t output anything. But we can source it: bash -c 'source ~/.zshrc; hello'. Or even: bash -c 'hello() { echo "hi"; }; declare -f'. Meaning: export will make things available for the current shell / children process, while source will bring variables only for the current shell. source changes only affect the current shell memory. export marks variables to be passed to child processes.
